The administration also assessed, with moderate confidence, that Araghchi's diplomatic faction lacked the command authority to translate a ceasefire framework into IRGCN Mosaic Defense stand-down orders regardless of what it committed to through the Saudi channel — a judgment confirmed by Araghchi's own March 1 admission — making a diplomatically expensive pause likely to produce no operationally meaningful Iranian behavioral change.

alternatives.label: Expand to Civilian Power Grid Targeting
alternatives.description: Under this alternative, the United States and Israel would have struck Iran's national electrical grid infrastructure — transmission stations, generation facilities, and the transformer network — simultaneously with or instead of the steelworks, implementing the power-plant obliteration threat Trump had issued on March 21 and maximizing civilian economic pressure in the most direct manner available. This approach would have been the logical execution of Trump's own publicly stated ultimatum and would have produced the most immediate and visible Iranian civilian pain of any available targeting option, including heat and cooling loss, water treatment failure, and hospital generator dependency across a population already experiencing wartime fuel rationing and 60-percent inflation. The operational argument for grid targeting over steelworks was that electrical infrastructure disruption is more rapidly reversible in principle — transformers can be replaced, grid sections restored — giving it a more credible coercive reversibility character than destroying permanent industrial capacity.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The NSC's judge advocate staff assessed civilian power grid attacks as crossing the threshold from dual-use military-economic targeting into civilian-harm-dominant infrastructure attacks under Additional Protocol I and customary international humanitarian law, generating an allied legal exposure that the administration calculated would cost it the European coalition remnants and potentially trigger ICJ provisional measures requests that seven current allies had privately indicated they would join or not oppose. The domestic political risk was assessed as even more acute: killing Iranian civilians through winter darkness and hospital generator failures while flying A-10s and Apaches and issuing sanctions waivers on Iranian crude oil would produce the single most incoherent strategic message of the entire conflict, and the Tucker Carlson 'Israel's war' framing would be amplified into a mass-casualty civilian harm narrative that the administration's six-vote Senate margin could not survive.

united_states.criteria_rationale: From the US perspective, acknowledged precision strikes against dual-use economic infrastructure with confirmed military procurement linkages, inflicting hundreds of casualties across three facilities, with mostly irreversible industrial destruction and significant norm violation in extending targeting to civilian workforce industrial plants — scoring lethality 3, attribution 3, target_sensitivity 2-3, geographic_scope 3, reversibility 3, norm_violation 2 — places the US at Level 4 rung 12, above limited overt military but not yet reaching strategic bombing of regime infrastructure.

iran.criteria_rationale: Iran's autonomous Mosaic Defense architecture has executed simultaneous ballistic missile campaigns against Israel and US bases, Hormuz mining, and Gulf state infrastructure strikes — sustained strategic conventional warfare across multiple theaters at lethality 3-4, attribution 1-2 (pre-delegated without political authorization), target_sensitivity 2-3, geographic_scope 3-4, reversibility 3-4, norm_violation 2-3 — placing Iran at rung 13, the upper bound of Major Conventional War.

israel.criteria_rationale: Israel's coordinated participation in the steelworks strikes, combined with its ongoing Lebanon ground invasion, Kharg Island oil infrastructure destruction, and South Pars gas processing strikes, constitutes sustained strategic conventional warfare against Iranian economic and military infrastructure at lethality 3, attribution 3, target_sensitivity 2-3, geographic_scope 3, reversibility 3, norm_violation 2 — placing Israel at rung 12 consistent with the US escalation level for the joint operation.

russia.criteria_rationale: Russia's confirmed Khayyam ISR real-time targeting support, drone guidance package transfer, and expanded intelligence sharing constitute materially consequential covert operational support that has enabled Iranian strikes killing US service members — attribution 1, lethality indirect, norm_violation 2-3 at the covert/deniable threshold — scoring at rung 6 within Level 2 given the direct operational targeting facilitation against US forces.

china.criteria_rationale: China's confirmed Caihong loitering munition delivery, sodium perchlorate shipment, and assessed missile component preparation constitute active covert military material support to an operational belligerent, with attribution 1-2 and norm_violation 1-2 — below Russia's direct operational ISR support but above commercial deniability — placing China at rung 5 within Level 2.

iran_perceives_us.rationale: From Tehran's perspective, systematic destruction of nuclear sites, supreme leadership decapitation, oil export terminal destruction, gas processing elimination, and now industrial base steelworks targeting constitutes strategic economic warfare against the Islamic Republic as an institution, perceived as rung 14 Level 5 strategic escalation regardless of US legal justification framing.

us_perceives_iran.rationale: US intelligence assesses Iran's Mosaic Defense autonomous ballistic missile campaigns, Hormuz mining, Gulf state infrastructure strikes, and Diego Garcia attack as upper-bound Major Conventional War, with the Diego Garcia reach and Fattah-2 hypersonic use generating Level 5 strategic escalation concern — US IC assesses rung 13 with moderate confidence given BDA uncertainty on remaining Iranian missile inventory.

israel_perceives_iran.rationale: Aman assesses Iran's demonstrated Fattah-2 hypersonic defeat of Arrow-3, Diego Garcia intermediate-range strike, Hezbollah front reactivation, and sustained strategic bombing of Israeli military infrastructure as strategic escalation regime, estimated at rung 14 with high confidence based on the qualitative capability thresholds Iran has demonstrably crossed.

events.title: Iranian drones strike Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia
events.description: Iranian drones and missiles targeted Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging several American aircraft including an E-3 Sentry AWACS and wounding multiple US service members. More than two dozen US troops were wounded in Iranian attacks on the base over the preceding week.
events.source_excerpt: Iranian drones and missiles targeted the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging several American aircraft — among them an E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft — and wounding multiple service members (ABC News).

full_briefing.situation: On March 24, 2026 — Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — Iranian drones and ballistic missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base, located approximately 130 kilometers southeast of Riyadh in Saudi Arabia, damaging several US aircraft including at least one E-3 Sentry AWACS airborne warning and control system aircraft and wounding multiple American service members. The strike is the most strategically significant Iranian attack on Saudi sovereign territory in the conflict's arc, hitting a facility whose airspace management and battle management functions are critical to CENTCOM's degraded Combined Air Operations Center architecture following the Al Udeid relocation. US personnel casualties are confirmed, with more than two dozen American service members wounded across Iranian attacks on Prince Sultan in the preceding week, establishing that the base has been under a sustained, deliberate targeting campaign rather than experiencing isolated opportunistic strikes. The AWACS damage is operationally consequential: E-3 Sentry aircraft provide the wide-area airspace surveillance, fighter coordination, and battle management integration that CENTCOM's carrier-aviation-dominant strike architecture depends on following the Gulf state basing denials, and their degradation compounds the IFF deconfliction vulnerability already demonstrated by the March 2 Kuwaiti Patriot fratricide of three US F-15Es.

actor_perspectives.united_states: CENTCOM's intelligence picture on the Prince Sultan strike is strong on attack vector and weapons characterization — signals collection and radar tracking confirm an Iranian IRGCN or IRGC Aerospace Force origin for the drone and missile salvo, and the targeting geometry confirms deliberate aim at the E-3 Sentry hardstands rather than incidental AWACS damage — but moderate-confidence only on the precise command authority that authorized the strike. The fundamental fog-of-war question is whether the attack was pre-loaded in Mosaic Defense provincial command packages and executed autonomously by surviving IRGC Aerospace Force or IRGCN provincial nodes, or whether Vahidi's warfighting cell issued a specific authorization in response to the March 24 steelworks strikes. CENTCOM J2 cannot resolve this distinction through available collection alone, and the distinction matters enormously: autonomous Mosaic Defense execution means there is no political authority in Tehran that could have prevented the strike regardless of Araghchi's diplomatic signaling, while Vahidi-cell authorization means there is a deliberate escalatory decision-making actor whose future choices might be shaped by coercive pressure or negotiated constraint. What the NSC's principals understand with high confidence is that the AWACS loss degrades the very battle management architecture that the conflict's air-sea operational concept requires, arriving at the moment that the War Powers margins are at their structural weakest and that domestic political tolerance for American casualties is being actively tested by the Republican isolationist caucus. The strike against Saudi sovereign territory also carries immediate diplomatic consequence: the January 2026 Gulf state basing denials were premised on the political calculation that neutrality could protect Gulf states from Iranian retaliation, and that premise has now been publicly falsified at Prince Sultan.
actor_perspectives.iran: Vahidi's IRGC warfighting cell and the surviving Mosaic Defense provincial commands are assessing the Prince Sultan strike as a doctrinal validation of the campaign's core targeting logic: US forward air operations capacity is being systematically degraded by targeting the command-and-control and battle management infrastructure rather than the individual combat platforms those assets support. The E-3 Sentry AWACS is precisely the category of target that Vahidi's preparation mandate identified as a force multiplier whose degradation imposes cascading operational costs across the entire US strike architecture, and striking it at Prince Sultan achieves a secondary coercive effect against Saudi Arabia's neutrality posture that the IRGC has been structuring into its Gulf infrastructure target packages since at least the January 2026 basing denial confirmed Riyadh's functional accommodation. From Araghchi's diplomatic faction's perspective, the Prince Sultan strike is a catastrophic compounding event: the faction has been attempting to signal diplomatic openness through the Saudi backchannel precisely because Saudi Arabia maintains a direct communication line to Mojtaba Khamenei's provisional government in Moscow, and an Iranian attack wounding American service members on Saudi sovereign territory simultaneously endangers the Saudi mediation posture and provides the Trump administration with a domestic political pretext to escalate rather than negotiate. The faction assesses, with high anxiety, that the strike will be interpreted in Washington as evidence that Iranian escalation continues regardless of any diplomatic signal, falsifying the premise underlying the backchannel's continued operation.
actor_perspectives.israel: Aman's assessment of the Prince Sultan strike is processed through two simultaneous analytical lenses: the operational and the diplomatic. Operationally, the AWACS damage at Prince Sultan is concerning for Israeli strike planning because E-3 Sentry battle management integration has been a component of the coordination architecture that CENTCOM and IDF air operations have shared since the conflict began, and its degradation imposes real-time deconfliction friction on the same carrier strike group coordination that the Lebanon ground campaign depends on for its own maritime fire support. Diplomatically, the Prince Sultan strike is the most acute direct test of Saudi Arabia's neutrality posture since the conflict began: if Riyadh absorbs an Iranian attack wounding American service members on its soil without formally recalibrating its relationship with Washington, it demonstrates the depth of MBS's commitment to independence from US operational demands; if Riyadh signals openness to US basing reconsideration in response to the attack, it collapses the January basing denial architecture that Iran's targeting calculus has been built around. Jerusalem's intelligence community is watching the Saudi reaction with the same intensity it is watching the AWACS battle damage assessment, because the Saudi response will determine whether the basing denial's strategic logic — which compressed US offensive options and extended Vahidi's warfighting window — remains durable or has been cracked by Iranian targeting that inadvertently made Saudi neutrality untenable.

full_briefing.context: The Prince Sultan strike on March 24 is the fourth distinct Iranian kinetic action against Gulf state or Gulf-hosted US assets within 23 days, following the March 1 Dubai IRGC strikes, the March 2 Ras Laffan attack, and the March 14 Fujairah terminal strike, establishing a systematic pattern of pressure against the Gulf neutrality architecture that the January basing denials institutionalized. The attack arrives in the same 24-hour window as the US-Israeli steelworks strikes against Mobarakeh, Khuzestan Steel, and Hormozgan Steel, creating a reciprocal escalation exchange in which each side's most recent action provides the political justification for the other's next move, a dynamic that the absence of any functional diplomatic channel — with the Qatari mediation destroyed by the Ras Laffan attack and the Saudi backchannel under severe strain — makes extremely difficult to interrupt. The AWACS degradation is particularly consequential because it directly compounds the March 2 Kuwaiti IFF fratricide deconfliction failure, both events pointing to the same systemic vulnerability: CENTCOM's battle management architecture is operating under sustained targeting pressure at precisely the moment its coordination capacity has been most reduced by the Al Udeid relocation, the Gulf state basing denials, and 25 days of accumulated operational tempo. Most consequentially for the simulation's forward arc, the strike against Prince Sultan has introduced a new political variable into Saudi Arabia's neutrality posture that MBS must now manage domestically and diplomatically: the January basing denial was premised on the calculation that withholding operational cooperation would protect Saudi territory from Iranian retaliation, and that calculation has now been publicly tested and found wanting, creating a decision point for Riyadh that is simultaneously an opportunity for Washington and a vulnerability for the Saudi-Chinese ceasefire mediation track.

chronicle.headline: Iranian Drones Hit Prince Sultan Air Base, Wound Dozens of Americans
chronicle.date_label: Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — March 24, 2026
chronicle.entry:
Iranian drones and ballistic missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on March 24, 2026, in the most operationally significant Iranian attack on a Gulf state military installation since Operation Epic Fury began on February 28. The strike damaged multiple US aircraft including at least one E-3 Sentry AWACS, the airborne command-and-control platform that coordinates fighter operations and provides wide-area radar coverage across CENTCOM's Arabian theater. More than two dozen American service members have been wounded in Iranian attacks on Prince Sultan over the preceding week, confirming that the base has been subjected to a deliberate, sustained targeting campaign rather than isolated harassment strikes. The AWACS damage represents the campaign's most significant degradation of US battle management capacity to date, striking a system whose loss cannot be quickly replaced and whose functions are irreplaceable in the current degraded basing environment following the Gulf state access denials of January 2026.

For the American service members at Prince Sultan, the past week has been defined by repeated attack, improvised shelter, and the specific dread of operating inside a facility that Iranian targeting cells have demonstrably calibrated their weapons to hit accurately. Medical personnel at the base have been managing a sustained casualty stream from drone and missile fragment wounds, burns, and blast injuries — the clinical texture of a military installation absorbing attritional attack rather than a single decisive strike. The E-3 Sentry crew and maintenance personnel whose aircraft was damaged represent a particular category of casualty: not combat aviators accepting mission risk over hostile territory, but airborne command-and-control specialists stationed at what was supposed to be a rear-area coordination node on Saudi sovereign territory, whose vulnerability there represents a fundamental failure of the force protection assumptions underlying the CENTCOM posture. The strike has also placed thousands of Saudi civilian and military personnel at Prince Sultan Base under Iranian attack for the third time this month, generating internal Saudi political pressure that the Al Thani government and MBS administration cannot absorb indefinitely without recalibrating the January basing denial's political calculus.

The diplomatic and economic consequences of the Prince Sultan strike radiate outward along several simultaneous tracks. For Saudi Arabia, the attack falsifies the protective premise of the January basing denial — that withholding US operational cooperation would insulate Riyadh from Iranian retaliation — creating immediate pressure on MBS to either formally protest Iran through a channel that risks collapsing the Saudi-Iranian backchannel ceasefire architecture, or absorb the attack in silence in ways that will generate domestic political cost and US diplomatic frustration simultaneously. For the Trump administration, the AWACS damage provides both a military operational problem — battle management degradation arriving as the Lebanon ground campaign and Hormuz mine-clearing operations compete for coordination bandwidth — and a domestic political opportunity to reframe the narrative from steelworks civilian targeting to American service members wounded on an allied state's soil. For the Zhai Jun ceasefire mediation tour, the strike adds a fresh complication: any framework that requires Saudi Arabian participation as a principal interlocutor now depends on a Riyadh that has just absorbed Iranian kinetic action against US forces on its territory, a political context that makes Saudi mediation posture harder to maintain without appearing complicit in Iranian violence against Americans.

Looking forward, the Prince Sultan strike establishes three forward-arc inflection points whose resolution will shape the conflict's next phase. First, Saudi Arabia's response — whether Riyadh signals basing reconsideration, formally protests Iran, or continues absorbing the attack in strategic silence — will determine whether the January basing denial's architecture survives as a functional constraint on US offensive options or begins to fracture under accumulated kinetic pressure. Second, the E-3 Sentry AWACS degradation forces an immediate CENTCOM decision about replacement rotation from the Diego Garcia-adjacent staging infrastructure that Iran demonstrated it can reach with intermediate-range ballistic missiles on March 20, creating a force protection dilemma with no clean solution. Third, the sustained American casualty rate at Prince Sultan — two dozen wounded in a week — is approaching the attritional threshold that Republican War Powers holdouts have identified as politically unsustainable, and each new casualty report provides the isolationist caucus with fresh factual content for the argument that American service members are absorbing Iranian attacks on Saudi soil for a conflict whose strategic rationale the administration cannot coherently articulate.

events.context_summary: On March 24, 2026 — Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — Iran struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia with drones and missiles, damaging at least one E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft and wounding US service members, representing the most consequential Iranian attack on a Gulf state military installation since February 28 and the fourth distinct IRGC kinetic action against Gulf or Gulf-hosted US assets in 23 days. The decision was Iran's IRGC Mosaic Defense provincial commands, acting under pre-delegated authority consistent with the autonomous operational pattern that Araghchi's faction publicly acknowledged on March 1, without confirmed authorization from Araghchi's diplomatic faction or Mojtaba Khamenei's provisional government in Moscow. The strike cost Saudi Arabia the protective premise of the January basing denial — that withholding US operational cooperation would shield Riyadh from Iranian targeting — and cost CENTCOM the E-3 Sentry battle management capacity whose degradation compounds the IFF deconfliction failures already demonstrated by the March 2 Kuwaiti Patriot fratricide. It changed the strategic landscape by simultaneously placing the Saudi neutrality architecture under its most acute kinetic test, degrading the US battle management infrastructure that the carrier-aviation-dominant operational concept requires, and adding fresh American casualty pressure to the six-vote Senate and seven-vote House War Powers margins that are the administration's sole legislative shield for continuing Epic Fury.

decision_analysis.decision_summary: Iran's IRGC Mosaic Defense provincial commands chose to strike Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia with drones and missiles, targeting US battle management assets and wounding American service members on Saudi sovereign territory.

alternatives.label: Continue Strikes on Israeli Targets Only
alternatives.description: Iran could have redirected the March 24 salvo package entirely toward Israeli military targets — Nevatim, Ramon, or northern command infrastructure supporting the Lebanon ground invasion — rather than extending the strike to Prince Sultan on Saudi territory. This option would have preserved the Iranian targeting logic of degrading Israeli military capacity while avoiding the acute diplomatic cost of attacking a base on the soil of a state whose January basing denial Iran has publicly praised and whose backchannel Saudi Arabia has been sustaining. The operational effect on US battle management would have been zero, but the political effect on the Saudi-Iranian diplomatic relationship and the ceasefire mediation architecture Araghchi's faction has been constructing through Riyadh would have been significantly better preserved. Israeli targets in the Negev remain within range of IRGC Aerospace Force provincial command assets in western Iran, and the March 24 targeting package could have been redirected toward Israeli ground force logistics supporting the Lebanon invasion.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The Prince Sultan targeting reflects Mosaic Defense pre-loaded packages rather than deliberate real-time strategic choice, and the provincial command nodes executing the strikes are operating on pre-finalized target lists that do not require or accept Araghchi's diplomatic faction input. Even if Araghchi's faction had the communication architecture to redirect the salvo — which it does not, as confirmed by Araghchi's own March 1 public acknowledgment of command fragmentation — the warfighting cell's pre-loaded packages prioritize US battle management assets as a force multiplication degradation objective that ranks above the diplomatic cost of striking Saudi territory. The autonomous execution architecture is producing the targeting outcome its designers intended, and the designers were Vahidi's preparation cell rather than the diplomatic faction whose ceasefire interests the Saudi-territory restriction would have served.

alternatives.label: Withhold Strike, Use Saudi Channel to Signal De-escalation
alternatives.description: Araghchi's diplomatic faction could have attempted — if it had the command authority it lacks — to stand down the Prince Sultan targeting package and use the Saudi backchannel to communicate Iranian restraint as a confidence-building measure supporting the ceasefire mediation framework that Zhai Jun's regional tour has been attempting to construct. This option would have preserved Saudi Arabia's dual-track neutrality posture and reinforced the premise of the Araghchi-MBS communication architecture: that Iran's diplomatic faction can deliver meaningful constraint on IRGC retaliatory activity in exchange for Saudi mediation services. The restraint signal, communicated through Riyadh to Washington, might have provided the Trump administration with a face-saving de-escalation opening at a moment when the steelworks strikes had generated maximum international criticism. The operational cost would have been the preservation of Prince Sultan's AWACS battle management capacity for US strike planning, but the diplomatic benefit would have been a potentially decisive advantage for Araghchi's faction in its internal contest with Vahidi's warfighting cell.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: Araghchi's faction demonstrably lacks the command authority to stand down pre-loaded Mosaic Defense provincial packages, a structural reality it publicly acknowledged on March 1 and that has been operationally confirmed by every subsequent autonomous IRGC strike. The faction's communication architecture through the Mashhad encrypted relay apparatus was partially compromised by the March 17 Larijani strikes that targeted the relay nodes, further degrading whatever residual coordination capacity might have reached IRGC Aerospace Force provincial commanders. Even if Araghchi's faction had transmitted a stand-down directive, the Mosaic Defense architecture was explicitly designed to execute pre-loaded packages regardless of political authority instructions that arrive during active operations, and the IRGC commanders executing the Prince Sultan salvo had no institutional obligation to honor a diplomatic faction directive that Vahidi's warfighting cell had not co-authorized.

alternatives.label: Escalate to Mass-Casualty Strike on US Forward Base
alternatives.description: Vahidi's warfighting cell held available the option of authorizing a substantially larger strike on Prince Sultan designed not merely to damage specific assets but to inflict mass American casualties — deploying the full salvo weight of available ballistic missiles against barracks, medical facilities, and command nodes rather than the precision-degradation logic applied against the AWACS hardstands. A mass-casualty strike on a US base would have constituted the clearest possible signal that Iran's autonomous command architecture can impose costs at the Black Hawk Down threshold that CENTCOM has identified as the domestic political override condition capable of forcing US operational pause. The weapons available to IRGC Aerospace Force western provincial commands include sufficient Fateh-313 and Zolfaghar ballistic missiles to saturate Prince Sultan's Patriot PAC-3 batteries and deliver a significant warhead yield against populated areas of the base, and this option would have imposed maximum American casualty pressure on the War Powers congressional margins.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The mass-casualty threshold represents a recognized escalation crossing that Iran's Mosaic Defense pre-loaded packages have been calibrated to approach without crossing, reflecting the same strategic logic that drove the selective Hormuz access regime rather than indiscriminate total closure. Vahidi's warfighting cell assesses that a mass-casualty strike on a US base — exceeding the 50-service-member Black Hawk Down threshold identified in US doctrine — would be the event most likely to collapse the War Powers congressional opposition and produce unified US escalatory authorization for options currently foreclosed by domestic political constraints, including potential ground operations or strikes against sites the US has thus far withheld from targeting. The cell's preferred calibration is maximum US operational cost below the mass-casualty trigger, sustaining the attritional pressure that is eroding War Powers margins without providing the single catastrophic event that would reset them.

united_states.criteria_rationale: US position is set by the March 24 steelworks strikes and the sustained carrier-based campaign — lethality at hundreds, attribution fully acknowledged, targets spanning dual-use industrial infrastructure, geographic scope across Iranian core territory, largely irreversible damage, and violation of precedented laws-of-armed-conflict norms on proportionality; the Prince Sultan attack does not change US rung but confirms the adversary engagement architecture.

iran.criteria_rationale: Iran's strike on Prince Sultan adds lethality at individual-to-dozens scale, attribution assessed as likely-to-confirmed from pre-delegated provincial commands, target sensitivity at military infrastructure with a high-value AWACS asset, geographic scope extending to third-party state sovereign territory hosting US forces, mostly irreversible damage to the AWACS, and gray-zone norm violation given that attacks on US forces in a third state that has denied basing authorization occupy ambiguous legal terrain; the compound effect of 25 days of autonomous strikes across multiple theaters maintains the rung-12 position.

israel.criteria_rationale: Israel's position is set by the March 24 steelworks strikes executed with US coordination, the ongoing Lebanon ground invasion, and the cumulative targeting of Iranian economic and military infrastructure across 25 days; the Prince Sultan event does not directly involve Israeli action but shapes the operational environment in which Israeli battle management coordination with CENTCOM now operates under degraded AWACS support.

russia.criteria_rationale: Russia's position reflects the Khayyam ISR targeting support and drone guidance package transfers confirmed on March 6 and 17 — lethality indirect, attribution publicly confirmed, target sensitivity oriented toward US military forces, geographic scope in existing conflict zone, partially irreversible in effect, and occupying a gray-zone norm violation through active targeting support to a belligerent attacking US forces.

china.criteria_rationale: China's position reflects the February 27 Caihong loitering munition delivery and March 2 sodium perchlorate shipment — lethality indirect, attribution assessed at likely, target sensitivity dual-use through commercial deniability, geographic scope at opponent non-core territory through transfer mechanism, partially reversible through supply-chain interdiction, and gray-zone norm violation through commercial cover for offensive capability transfer.

iran_perceives_us.rationale: Iran's provincial command nodes and Vahidi's warfighting cell assess US actions through accumulated battle damage — steelworks, Kharg Island, coastal suppression, Diego Garcia ISR — placing US at the upper end of Major Conventional War with observed movement toward strategic economic destruction that the IRGC's internal assessments score above the US's own legal characterization.

us_perceives_iran.rationale: US intelligence assesses Iran at the Major Conventional War rung based on 25 days of sustained autonomous multi-domain strikes, the Hormuz mine-laying, and attacks across third-party state sovereign territory, but the command fragmentation confirmed by Araghchi on March 1 reduces confidence in distinguishing deliberate escalatory decisions from autonomous package execution.

israel_perceives_iran.rationale: Aman scores Iran higher than US intelligence due to the Fattah-2 hypersonic debut on March 1, the Diego Garcia intermediate-range strike on March 20, and the systematic targeting of Gulf economic infrastructure, assessing these as evidence of a deliberate strategic escalation logic that the autonomous execution framing may understate.

events.title: Israel announces Lebanon home demolition campaign
events.description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz stated the IDF would destroy homes in southern Lebanon using the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' from Gaza, to eliminate Hezbollah militants. This signaled a significant escalation of the ground invasion launched March 16.
events.source_excerpt: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said the Israeli military will destroy homes in southern Lebanon, just as it did in the war-torn Gaza Strip (ABC News).

full_briefing.situation: On March 24, 2026 — Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz publicly announced that the Israel Defense Forces would systematically demolish homes in southern Lebanon using what he explicitly described as the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' imported from the Gaza campaign, signaling a formal doctrinal expansion of Israel's ground invasion launched eight days earlier on March 16. The announcement is not a discrete strike event but a policy declaration with immediate operational implications: IDF engineering units and D9 armored bulldozers already deployed south of the Litani River are now operating under a ministerially authorized demolition mandate rather than case-by-case military necessity justifications, and the language suggests a systematic clearance campaign of Lebanese residential structures in areas assessed as Hezbollah fighting positions or weapons storage infrastructure. The 91st Division's three-committed armored and infantry brigades are simultaneously engaging Hezbollah defensive belt positions between the Blue Line and the Litani while engineering and logistics elements prepare the physical demolition infrastructure the campaign requires. The announcement arrives on the same day as the IRGC's drone and missile strike on Prince Sultan Air Base that wounded American service members and damaged at least one E-3 Sentry AWACS, creating a simultaneous bilateral escalation exchange in which Iran's autonomous Mosaic Defense architecture and Israel's deliberate policy declaration are advancing the conflict's destructive footprint on parallel tracks within the same 24-hour operational window.

actor_perspectives.united_states: CENTCOM and the NSC are processing the Katz announcement through two simultaneous analytical lenses whose conclusions pull in opposite directions. Operationally, the demolition campaign provides the IDF's ground invasion with a structural clearance logic that reduces Hezbollah's ability to reconstitute defensive positions behind IDF lines, and this operational benefit partially justifies the political cost — but CENTCOM J2 is acutely aware that the 'Rafah model' reference is the single phrase most likely to accelerate congressional revolt against the War Powers margins already reduced to six votes in the Senate and seven in the House, because it invokes the Gaza imagery that has already cost the administration significant domestic political capital. The intelligence picture on the demolition campaign's likely human displacement scale is assessed at high confidence: the Gaza application of this model produced mass displacement and international legal condemnation at a scale that strained the Abraham Accords framework, and Lebanon's population density south of the Litani — with villages like Khiam, Bint Jbeil, and Aita al-Shaab in the operational zone — creates an equivalent or worse displacement trajectory. The NSC's principals are not receiving advance notification of Israeli operational decisions as a matter of consistent pattern, meaning the Katz announcement was processed from public media rather than through the US-Israel military coordination channel, and the absence of prior consultation is itself a strategic data point that the administration must manage domestically against the Republican isolationist caucus's 'Israel's war' framing that the Kent resignation, the Rubio walkback, and the Kharg Island credit dispute have all amplified. What Washington cannot resolve through available collection is whether Katz's statement represents a genuine operational order already in execution or a coercive signaling instrument designed to accelerate Hezbollah's withdrawal from forward defensive positions — a distinction whose answer will determine whether the diplomatic cost lands in the next 48 hours or is deferred by military developments.
actor_perspectives.iran: Vahidi's IRGC warfighting cell and the surviving Mosaic Defense provincial commands are assessing the Katz home demolition announcement through the lens of its effect on the Lebanese front's operational sustainability and on the Axis of Resistance's mobilization narrative. From the warfighting cell's perspective, the demolition campaign represents an Israeli strategic overextension that will deepen the IDF's logistical and personnel commitment in Lebanon on a timeline that compresses Israel's ability to respond to simultaneous Iranian ballistic missile pressure, and the 'Rafah model' framing provides the IRGC Quds Force's liaison cell in the Bekaa Valley with its most effective mobilization argument since the March 2 Hezbollah front reactivation — specifically, the imagery of Lebanese homes being systematically bulldozed will accelerate Hezbollah rank-and-file resistance and reduce the internal pressure on Hezbollah's post-Nasrallah military command to accept any ceasefire framework that leaves Israeli armor on Lebanese soil. Araghchi's diplomatic faction, operating from encrypted relay facilities in Mashhad, assesses the Katz announcement as a catastrophic compounding event for the Saudi-Chinese ceasefire mediation architecture: the announcement destroys whatever residual Lebanese domestic political space existed for a negotiated Hezbollah withdrawal, eliminates the diplomatic fig leaf that a UN Resolution 1701-plus framework could have provided, and ensures that the Lebanese government's ceasefire interlocutor role — which Saudi Arabia's backchannel had been attempting to activate — is politically untenable as long as IDF bulldozers are visible on Lebanese television. The faction's most acute anxiety is that the demolition campaign, by foreclosing Lebanese political participation in any near-term ceasefire framework, has effectively removed the one diplomatic track whose progress did not depend on resolving the Mosaic Defense autonomous command authority problem.
actor_perspectives.israel: Jerusalem's security cabinet and IDF General Staff are managing the Katz announcement simultaneously as an operational enablement decision and a political liability whose magnitude they are calibrated to accept. Operationally, the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' reference reflects a genuine IDF assessment that the Gaza clearance methodology — however costly in diplomatic terms — produced measurable degradation of Hamas tunnel and fighting position infrastructure, and that Hezbollah's southern Lebanon defensive architecture, built over two decades of IRGC-funded construction and extensively documented by Unit 8200 and Aman, contains an equivalent density of residential-structure-integrated military assets that cannot be neutralized through precision strikes alone without the physical clearance that demolition provides. Aman's assessment of the political cost is processed against a domestic Israeli security logic that has been running since March 2: Hezbollah's March 2 reactivation under pre-delegated Mosaic Defense authority fired approximately 100 rockets and short-range ballistic missiles into northern Israel at an interceptor expenditure rate that Iron Dome cannot sustain indefinitely, and the security cabinet's calculation is that the Lebanese population displacement that demolition generates is militarily preferable to the Israeli civilian risk that an indefinite Hezbollah rocket campaign imposes on Galilee and Haifa communities already absorbing Iranian ballistic missile pressure. The diplomatic dimension Aman is most closely tracking is the Lebanese front's interaction with the Arrow-3 resupply authorization timeline: if the demolition campaign accelerates Hezbollah's operational degradation sufficiently to reduce the northern front's interceptor consumption rate within the next ten to fourteen days, it partially relieves the depletion arithmetic that is the conflict's most time-sensitive constraint. The Lapid no-confidence motion filed on March 16 and Golan's 'this government does not know how to win wars' framing are the domestic political reference points against which the security cabinet is measuring the demolition announcement's internal sustainability.

full_briefing.context: The Katz home demolition announcement is the operational articulation of the strategic logic that drove the March 16 Lebanon ground invasion decision, explicitly importing the Gaza campaign's most internationally condemned methodology into a new theater at the precise moment the conflict's cumulative diplomatic, political, and military pressures are all approaching simultaneous threshold conditions. The announcement arrives on Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury in a context defined by the convergence of the March 24 steelworks strikes against Mobarakeh, Khuzestan Steel, and Hormozgan Steel — themselves the most legally contested targeting authorization the campaign has produced — and the same-day Prince Sultan AWACS attack that has placed the Saudi neutrality architecture under its most acute kinetic test, creating a three-directional escalation moment in which Israeli ground operations in Lebanon, US-Israeli air strikes against Iranian industrial infrastructure, and Iranian autonomous Mosaic Defense strikes against Gulf-hosted US assets are all advancing simultaneously without a functional diplomatic channel to absorb any of them. The demolition campaign's 'Rafah model' framing is specifically significant in the context of the Lebanese front's relationship to the broader Arc of Resistance: Hezbollah's March 2 reactivation was executed under IRGC Quds Force pre-delegated authorization codes transmitted by surviving Mosaic Defense provincial nodes, meaning the Lebanese front is structurally integrated into Iran's autonomous retaliatory architecture rather than independently commanded, and a demolition campaign that displaces the civilian population whose protection is Hezbollah's core legitimacy claim creates a mobilization dynamic that the IRGC's pre-loaded operational sequencing was explicitly designed to exploit. Most consequentially for the simulation's forward arc, the Katz announcement establishes a political timeline constraint that operates independently of the kinetic and diplomatic pressures already saturating NSC decision-making bandwidth: the demolition campaign's images will circulate globally on a days-to-weeks timeline, accelerating pressure on the War Powers congressional margins from the Republican isolationist caucus whose 47 Senate and 212 House votes are the administration's only legislative shield, while simultaneously foreclosing the Lebanese political participation pathway that Saudi Arabia's backchannel had been attempting to construct as a ceasefire framework element that did not require resolving the Araghchi-Vahidi succession contest or Mojtaba Khamenei's Russian custody situation.

chronicle.headline: Israel Announces Lebanon Home Demolition Using Gaza Model
chronicle.date_label: Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — March 24, 2026
chronicle.entry:
Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz announced on March 24 that the IDF would systematically demolish homes in southern Lebanon using the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' pioneered during the Gaza campaign, formalizing a clearance doctrine for the ground invasion launched eight days earlier on March 16. The announcement was not an operational report of isolated demolitions but a policy declaration authorizing IDF engineering units and armored bulldozers already deployed south of the Litani River to conduct systematic residential structure clearance across villages assessed as integrated into Hezbollah's defensive belt infrastructure. The 91st Division's committed brigades are simultaneously engaging Hezbollah positions between the Blue Line and the Litani while the declaration signals a sustained multi-week clearance campaign whose geographic scope extends across a densely populated strip containing towns including Khiam, Bint Jbeil, Maroun al-Ras, and Aita al-Shaab — communities with combined populations in the hundreds of thousands who had not fully returned since the 2024 ceasefire. The decision arrived on the same day Iranian IRGC autonomous Mosaic Defense commands struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia and US-Israeli strikes destroyed three major Iranian steelworks, embedding the demolition announcement within the conflict's most escalation-saturated single calendar day.

The human dimension of the Katz announcement is not yet a casualty count but a displacement trajectory whose scale is measurable from the Gaza precedent being explicitly invoked. The 'Beit Hanoun model' refers to the systematic clearance of a northern Gaza town whose residential structures were demolished block by block as IDF forces advanced, producing complete displacement of its population and international legal proceedings before the ICJ that became a defining image of the Gaza campaign's most condemned phase. Applied to the villages south of the Litani — whose largely Shia civilian population had been living under Hezbollah's political and social infrastructure for two decades — the demolition mandate will produce Lebanese families photographed fleeing with belongings as Israeli bulldozers level structures behind them, images whose international circulation is immediate and whose political half-life in Western capitals is measured in weeks rather than days. The IDF is operating under a military necessity legal framing that asserts the residential structures being demolished serve as Hezbollah weapons storage, tunnel entrances, and fighting positions — a framing that Unit 8200 target development files support in specific cases and that the Gaza precedent has established as legally contested but operationally practiced, leaving the announcement's domestic and international legal exposure as a deferred cost rather than an immediate operational constraint.

The diplomatic and economic consequences of the demolition announcement compound an already saturated escalation environment in ways that interact specifically with the conflict's most critical pending decisions. The 'Rafah model' reference immediately activates the international legal and humanitarian reaction architecture that has been dormant on the Lebanon front since the March 16 invasion's initial 886-plus Lebanese deaths and one million displaced — UN OCHA emergency protocols, ICRC access demands, and European foreign ministry statement cycles that impose incremental diplomatic cost on Washington's already strained allied relationships. The Republican isolationist caucus whose 47 Senate votes and 212 House votes constitute the near-majority already within reach of defeating a War Powers Resolution will process the demolition images through the same political frame that the Gaza ceasefire collapse, the January massacre, and the Kharg Island destruction established, accelerating the timeline to a potential legislative constraint without requiring any additional Iranian escalatory action to provide political cover. Most consequentially for the ceasefire architecture that Saudi Arabia and China's Zhai Jun tour have been attempting to construct, the demolition campaign forecloses Lebanese government participation in any near-term diplomatic framework by making the Lebanese state's public association with a ceasefire that leaves IDF bulldozers operating in its territory a domestic political impossibility — eliminating the one diplomatic track whose viability did not depend on resolving the Araghchi-Vahidi succession contest or Russian custody of Mojtaba Khamenei.

Looking forward, the demolition campaign establishes a new operational variable whose interaction with the conflict's existing pressure vectors creates compounding risk on multiple simultaneous timelines. The IDF's ground commitment south of the Litani is now deepening on both a kinetic and an infrastructure clearance track, meaning Israel's military bandwidth is being consumed by a sustained two-front commitment — Iranian ballistic missiles and Hezbollah ground resistance — at the precise moment that the Arrow-3 resupply authorization, the Hormuz MCM timeline, and the steelworks strikes' legal and diplomatic fallout are all approaching simultaneous decision points. The demolition campaign's success, measured in IDF terms, requires three to five weeks of sustained operations against Hezbollah defensive belts whose depth and construction quality the 2006 war and the November 2024 ceasefire's un-enforced provisions had preserved at near-intact levels, creating an operational timeline that extends well beyond any ceasefire framework that could plausibly be assembled within the Chinese strategic reserve depletion window or the War Powers congressional margin's durability. The Lapid no-confidence motion and Golan's war-competence challenge provide the domestic Israeli political pressure instrument against which the demolition campaign's progress will be measured, and if IDF forces encounter sustained Hezbollah resistance enabled by IRGC pre-loaded defensive position intelligence — as occurred in the 2006 war's most costly phases — the Katz announcement will retrospectively define the moment when Israel's strategic overextension became irreversible.

events.context_summary: On March 24, 2026 — Day 25 of Operation Epic Fury — Israeli Defense Minister Katz announced the IDF would demolish homes in southern Lebanon using the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' from the Gaza campaign, formalizing a systematic residential clearance doctrine for the ground invasion launched March 16 and authorizing IDF engineering units already south of the Litani River to conduct block-by-block demolition across villages in the operational zone. The decision was Netanyahu's security cabinet's, driven by IDF assessment that Hezbollah's defensive belt infrastructure is integrated into residential structures at a density requiring physical clearance that precision strikes alone cannot achieve, executed without US advance notification and in deliberate doctrinal continuity with the Gaza campaign's most internationally condemned methodology. The announcement cost Israel its remaining Lebanese civilian protection narrative and foreclosed Lebanese government participation in any near-term ceasefire framework, simultaneously activating international legal and humanitarian reaction cycles and accelerating the Republican isolationist caucus's War Powers challenge timeline by delivering the 'Rafah model' imagery that its domestic political framing requires. It changed the strategic landscape by converting the Lebanese front from a fire-support and attrition operation into a sustained demolition-clearance campaign on a three-to-five-week operational timeline that extends beyond every existing ceasefire mediation window, deepens Israel's two-front military commitment at the conflict's most pressure-saturated moment, and establishes an Israeli doctrinal precedent whose political and legal costs will compound independently of the kinetic and diplomatic pressures already saturating NSC and War Powers decision-making bandwidth.

decision_analysis.decision_summary: Israel chose to formally announce a systematic home demolition campaign in southern Lebanon modeled on Gaza clearance operations.

alternatives.label: Limited Precision Clearance — Case-by-Case Military Necessity
alternatives.description: Israel could have authorized IDF engineering units to conduct demolitions on a case-by-case basis under existing military necessity doctrine without a formal ministerial announcement, maintaining operational clearance capacity while avoiding the public invocation of the 'Rafah model' that transforms each individual demolition from a contested tactical decision into a confirmed systematic policy. This approach would have achieved much of the same physical infrastructure clearance over a longer timeline by targeting specific structures with confirmed Hezbollah weapons storage or tunnel infrastructure rather than block-by-block residential clearance, and it would have preserved international legal ambiguity — the difference between individual military necessity demolitions and an explicitly announced systematic clearance campaign is precisely the distinction that ICJ proceedings, ICRC documentation standards, and congressional war powers debates turn on. The approach would have maintained Lebanese government participation in ceasefire mediation by preserving the political fiction that IDF ground operations were targeting Hezbollah infrastructure rather than the civilian residential fabric that is Lebanon's political sovereignty in physical form, giving Riyadh and Beijing's mediation architecture a Lebanese interlocutor to work with rather than a government whose domestic legitimacy is destroyed by association with a publicly declared demolition campaign.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The case-by-case alternative was rejected because Netanyahu's security cabinet assessed that the operational tempo required to degrade Hezbollah's southern Lebanon defensive belt within the three-to-five-week window that Israeli interceptor arithmetic and Iranian ballistic missile pressure impose cannot be sustained without the systematic clearance methodology that a formal ministerial authorization enables — individual military necessity demolitions require legal review at brigade and division level that slows the engineering tempo to a rate incompatible with the operational timeline. Additionally, the security cabinet's domestic political logic favored the explicit policy announcement because it provides the IDF's ground forces with clear operational authority that prevents frontline commander hesitation, signals to Hezbollah's fighting units that the clearance mandate is unconditional and non-negotiable, and — in Netanyahu's domestic political framing — demonstrates the wartime decisiveness that Lapid's no-confidence motion and Golan's competence challenge were designed to undermine.

alternatives.label: Operational Pause — Diplomacy-First Lebanese Track
alternatives.description: Israel could have paused offensive ground operations south of the Litani for 72 to 96 hours and authorized a ceasefire exploratory dialogue through UNIFIL, the Lebanese Armed Forces, and Saudi Arabia's backchannel, framing the pause as a humanitarian corridor for civilian evacuation rather than a strategic concession, with the demolition decision deferred pending Hezbollah's response to IDF demands for withdrawal from designated defensive positions. This approach would have preserved Lebanese government participation in the Saudi-Chinese ceasefire mediation architecture by creating political space for Beirut to act as a nominal ceasefire interlocutor, given Araghchi's diplomatic faction a Lebanese front de-escalatory signal to present to the Riyadh mediation framework, and reduced the War Powers congressional margin pressure on Washington by removing the demolition imagery from the 24-hour news cycle during the conflict's most politically vulnerable week. The approach was technically feasible given that the 91st Division's committed brigades had only been in contact with Hezbollah's forward defensive positions for eight days and had not yet encountered the depth of the defensive belt that would make a tactical pause operationally costly.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The diplomatic pause alternative was rejected because the security cabinet assessed that a 72-to-96-hour operational halt would allow Hezbollah to reconstitute forward defensive positions, redistribute weapons from structures identified by IDF targeting intelligence, and reposition anti-tank guided missile crews that have been the primary cause of IDF casualties in the first eight days of ground contact — the operational cost of a pause outweighs its diplomatic benefit when Hezbollah's organizational capacity to exploit pauses is assessed at high confidence from 2006 and 2024 precedent. The domestic political constraint is equally decisive: Lapid's no-confidence motion and Golan's 'this government does not know how to win wars' framing have created a political environment in which any pause that can be characterized as hesitation or strategic uncertainty adds credibility to the opposition's competence challenge, and Netanyahu's security cabinet is acutely sensitive to the domestic appearance of weakness at the conflict's most militarily demanding moment.

alternatives.label: Targeted Leadership Decapitation — Hezbollah Command Strike
alternatives.description: Israel could have substituted the demolition campaign announcement with a precision decapitation strike package against Hezbollah's post-Nasrallah military command structure in the Bekaa Valley — targeting the IRGC Quds Force liaison cell and the surviving leadership tier that has been directing operations since the March 2 Mosaic Defense reactivation — using the same long-range precision architecture that killed twelve Houthi Cabinet members in August 2025 and enabled the Khamenei strike in Operation Roaring Lion. This approach would have addressed the military problem — Hezbollah rocket and missile fire that is depleting Iron Dome interceptors at an unsustainable rate — through a command degradation methodology rather than a residential clearance methodology, preserving the civilian population's political utility as a Lebanese government mediation asset while imposing a leadership continuity cost on Hezbollah that the physical demolition of southern villages cannot achieve. The approach would have been internationally defensible under the same military targeting precedents that established the Houthi Cabinet and IRGC leadership strikes as lawful under Israeli military doctrine.
alternatives.why_not_chosen: The decapitation strike alternative was assessed as insufficient to address the operational problem that the demolition campaign is designed to solve, because Hezbollah's post-Nasrallah military structure — already adapted to IRGC pre-delegated command authority after two years of Israeli leadership targeting — has demonstrated a resilience to command decapitation that the March 2 Mosaic Defense reactivation confirmed at high confidence. The IDF's targeting intelligence on Hezbollah's Bekaa Valley command nodes is assessed at moderate confidence after nineteen months of sustained collection since October 2023, but the dispersed cell structure adopted following the October 2024 Nasrallah killing means that no single strike package can achieve the command degradation that a sustained systematic clearance of the physical infrastructure sustaining Hezbollah's southern Lebanon forward operating capacity can accomplish over three to five weeks. Additionally, the security cabinet's ground invasion logic is explicitly premised on physical territorial control rather than command degradation — Netanyahu's stated objective of seizing all territory south of the Litani requires physical presence and infrastructure clearance that air and precision strike operations cannot substitute for.

united_states.criteria_rationale: US has conducted strategic bombing of Iranian economic infrastructure including steelworks and oil export terminals, executed coastal suppression strikes at Bandar Abbas, and is managing sustained base attacks across the CENTCOM theater — geographic scope extending to Iranian core territory, lethality in the hundreds, and target sensitivity extending to dual-use economic infrastructure places the US firmly in Level 4 Major Conventional War at the upper end.

iran.criteria_rationale: Iran's Mosaic Defense autonomous commands have struck Saudi sovereign territory at Prince Sultan Air Base wounding US service members, maintained Hormuz mining operations, struck Diego Garcia, fired Fattah-2 hypersonic vehicles against Israeli command facilities, and conducted sustained mass missile and drone campaigns against US forward bases across the region — lethality in the hundreds cumulatively, geographic scope extending to Indian Ocean territories, and infrastructure targeting at dual-use and economic levels sustain Level 4.

israel.criteria_rationale: Israel has now added systematic home demolition doctrine in Lebanon to a campaign that already includes strategic destruction of Iranian oil export infrastructure at Kharg Island and South Pars, a major ground invasion of Lebanon, and coordinated steelworks strikes — the demolition campaign's target sensitivity (civilian/infrastructure), geographic scope (Lebanese sovereign territory), and largely irreversible physical destruction advance the rung to the upper end of Level 4.

russia.criteria_rationale: Russia is providing real-time Khayyam satellite targeting data and improved drone guidance packages to IRGC commands — attribution is suspected-to-likely but not publicly acknowledged by Moscow, the actions are covert support rather than direct combat, and while the norm violation is serious (targeting data for strikes against US personnel), Russia's own military forces have not engaged directly, sustaining Level 2.

china.criteria_rationale: China has delivered Caihong loitering munitions, sodium perchlorate oxidizer shipments, and assessed missile component support to Iran — attribution is suspected-to-likely with commercial deniability maintained, norm violation is significant but precedented in gray-zone arms transfers, and China's own forces have not directly engaged, placing it at Level 2 lower rung.

iran_perceives_us.rationale: Iran's provincial commands and Vahidi's warfighting cell have direct battlefield evidence of US strategic bombing against economic infrastructure, Bandar Abbas coastal suppression strikes, Diego Garcia staging for B-2 nuclear-facility-capable strikes, and Kharg Island obliteration — Iranian intelligence perceives US at Strategic Escalation level targeting economic and regime-sustaining infrastructure.

us_perceives_iran.rationale: US intelligence has high-confidence BDA on Iranian ballistic missile campaigns, Hormuz mining, Diego Garcia strikes, and Fattah-2 hypersonic employment, but moderate confidence only on the precise authorization chain between Mosaic Defense autonomous commands and Vahidi's warfighting cell — the command authority ambiguity reduces confidence on escalation intent versus autonomous execution.

israel_perceives_iran.rationale: Aman assesses Iran at the higher end of Major Conventional War approaching Strategic Escalation based on confirmed Fattah-2 hypersonic strikes against fortified IDF command facilities, sustained mass missile campaigns, and Hezbollah front reactivation under pre-delegated IRGC authority — but Aman's confidence is moderated by the unresolved question of Mosaic Defense autonomous execution versus deliberate Vahidi-cell escalation decisions.

events.title: US delivers 15-point peace plan via Pakistan; Iran rejects
events.description: The US delivered a peace plan through Pakistani mediators demanding Iran shutter its nuclear program, end ballistic missile production, and open the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for discussing sanctions reduction. Iran rejected the plan and offered a counterproposal demanding reparations and sovereignty recognition over Hormuz.
events.source_excerpt: The US plan demanded that Iran shutter its nuclear program, end ballistic missile production, and open the Strait of Hormuz in return for discussing the potential reduction of sanctions (The National Interest).

full_briefing.situation: On March 25, 2026 — Day 26 of Operation Epic Fury — the United States delivered a comprehensive 15-point peace framework to Iran through Pakistani diplomatic intermediaries, with Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar serving as the primary channel between US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff's team and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's diplomatic faction, now operating from encrypted relay facilities in Mashhad following the destruction of the Qatari mediation channel and the March 17 killing of Ali Larijani. The US plan demands that Iran permanently shutter its nuclear program, halt all ballistic missile production, and fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz — demands that would strip Tehran of its three primary strategic deterrence pillars simultaneously — in exchange for entering discussions about potential sanctions reduction, a formulation that offers no guaranteed economic relief and no security guarantee against future military action. Iran's response, delivered within hours through the same Pakistani channel, formally rejected all three demands and presented a counterproposal requiring the United States to pay reparations for the destruction inflicted since June 2025, issue a formal sovereignty recognition of Iran's jurisdiction over the Strait of Hormuz, and acknowledge Iranian rights to a civilian nuclear program — terms that effectively demand the US legitimize rather than reverse the strategic conditions the Mosaic Defense campaign has created. The diplomatic exchange is occurring in a context of continued active military operations: Israeli ground forces remain south of the Litani River in Lebanon, IRGCN autonomous mine-laying operations continue to enforce the Hormuz closure against Western shipping, Iranian Mosaic Defense provincial commands retain pre-delegated retaliatory authority, and US carrier strike groups Abraham Lincoln and Gerald R. Ford remain in the Arabian Sea and Red Sea respectively with their combined air wings conducting daily suppression missions against Iranian coastal command infrastructure.

actor_perspectives.united_states: The NSC's analytical picture of the Pakistani channel delivery is mixed at the intelligence collection level: Witkoff's team has high confidence that the 15-point framework was physically transmitted to Araghchi's faction through Dar's intermediation, but faces fundamental uncertainty about whether Araghchi's diplomatic faction retains sufficient command authority within the Islamic Republic's fractured post-Epic-Fury governance structure to deliver any concessions even if it wished to accept them — an uncertainty that the March 1 acknowledgment of autonomous Mosaic Defense operations and the subsequent Larijani killing have rendered structurally unresolvable through available collection. US intelligence assesses with moderate confidence that Pakistan's decision to serve as intermediary reflects Islamabad's calculation that its nuclear-armed status and its historical role as an Iran-US back-channel dating to the 1980s provides both the diplomatic cover and the strategic inducement to engage, but the NSC cannot rule out that Iranian intelligence services have briefed the Pakistani ISI on Araghchi's actual authority limitations in ways that make the channel a communication conduit rather than a genuine negotiating forum. The administration's simultaneous deployment of GL 135's Iranian crude waiver, the 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum, and the Truth Social wind-down post — all issued within 72 hours of this diplomatic delivery — means that the 15-point framework arrives in Tehran surrounded by mutually contradictory US signaling that every Iranian analytical cell, including Araghchi's faction, will assess as evidence of a divided and domestically constrained principal rather than a coherent coercive diplomatic strategy. What CENTCOM J2 and the CIA's Iran Mission Center cannot determine from available collection is whether Iran's counterproposal — demanding reparations and Hormuz sovereignty recognition — was authorized by Vahidi's IRGC warfighting cell as a deliberate rejection designed to extend the conflict's operational phase, or whether it represents Araghchi's faction's genuine opening position in a negotiation that the faction intends to pursue over Pakistani objections to the warfighting cell.
actor_perspectives.iran: Araghchi's diplomatic faction, operating from Mashhad under maximum communications security following the March 17 compromise of the relay apparatus that killed Larijani, received the 15-point framework through the Pakistani channel and assessed it within hours as structurally incompatible with any political settlement the faction could bring before Vahidi's warfighting cell or Mojtaba Khamenei's nominal government in Moscow without immediately destroying whatever residual credibility the diplomatic faction retains within the Islamic Republic's fractured command structure. The three core demands — nuclear program termination, ballistic missile production halt, and full Hormuz reopening — collectively require Iran to surrender the three deterrence instruments whose retention is the non-negotiable minimum that Vahidi's cell has publicly committed to defending, meaning any acceptance would constitute a political capitulation that would hand Vahidi the succession argument he needs to permanently marginalize Araghchi's faction from any future governance role. Iran's counterproposal demanding reparations and Hormuz sovereignty recognition was calibrated not as a serious negotiating position but as a diplomatic response that signals ongoing engagement through the Pakistani channel — preserving the communication conduit Islamabad represents — while establishing maximalist public terms that demonstrate to the Iranian domestic audience and to the warfighting cell that the diplomatic faction is not conceding under pressure. Vahidi's warfighting cell's assessment of the US framework is more straightforward: it reads the three simultaneous demands as confirmation that the Trump administration is negotiating from domestic political distress rather than military superiority, and that the correct response is to sustain the Mosaic Defense autonomous operational architecture while China's resupply logistics extend the IRGC's munitions reconstitution timeline beyond the operational ceiling that US war planners had assumed when authorizing Epic Fury.
actor_perspectives.israel: Jerusalem's security cabinet and Aman directorate are tracking the Pakistani channel diplomatic exchange primarily through the lens of its interaction with the Lebanon ground invasion's operational timeline and the Arrow-3 resupply authorization decision that remains the conflict's most time-sensitive Israeli constraint. Israel was not a party to the 15-point framework's drafting — the NSC did not coordinate the document's content with Netanyahu's security cabinet before delivery through Pakistani intermediaries, continuing the pattern of US diplomatic initiatives executed without advance Israeli consultation that has characterized the conflict since the February 6 Muscat reopening — and the security cabinet's initial reaction, conveyed through the US-Israel military coordination channel rather than public statement, is that the framework's demand for a halt to ballistic missile production represents the single most valuable concession Iran could make from Israel's threat calculus perspective and should not be traded away for anything less than full, verifiable, IAEA-inspected nuclear program dismantlement with unlimited inspection access. Aman's assessment of Iran's counterproposal — demanding reparations and sovereignty recognition over Hormuz — is that it constitutes a deliberate diplomatic non-starter issued from Araghchi's faction specifically to demonstrate to Vahidi's warfighting cell that the diplomatic faction cannot be accused of softness, and that the same counterproposal functions as a signal to Pakistan and to international mediators that Iran's diplomatic faction remains active and engaged even as its autonomous Mosaic Defense commands continue kinetic operations. What Israel's intelligence apparatus cannot resolve through available collection is whether the Pakistani channel represents a genuine US diplomatic effort to construct a ceasefire architecture before the Arrow-3 resupply decision forces Washington to acknowledge Israeli dependency publicly, or whether the 15-point delivery was a domestic political exercise designed to demonstrate negotiating good faith before an anticipated third round of US-Israeli strikes against Iran's remaining hydrocarbon and military infrastructure.

full_briefing.context: The US delivery of a 15-point peace framework through Pakistani intermediaries on March 25 represents the first structured American diplomatic initiative since the destruction of the Qatari mediation channel by the March 2 Ras Laffan strike and the March 17 compromise of Araghchi's Mashhad relay apparatus through the Larijani killing, arriving on Day 26 of Operation Epic Fury in a strategic environment defined by the simultaneous forward motion of incompatible pressures: Israeli ground forces demolishing southern Lebanese villages under the 'Rafah model' doctrine authorized on March 24, IRGCN autonomous mining operations enforcing a 60-to-90-day physical Hormuz closure that no diplomatic agreement can accelerate, Mojtaba Khamenei's nominal supreme authority sequestered in Moscow under conditions that make ceasefire authentication dependent on Russian co-authorization, and a US domestic political architecture whose six-vote Senate and seven-vote House War Powers margins are being compressed by the daily accumulation of base casualties, energy price shocks, and allied diplomatic ruptures. Pakistan's selection as the intermediary channel reflects the exhaustion of all more proximate options — Oman's credibility was permanently damaged by the February 26 'within reach' declaration's forty-eight-hour juxtaposition with Epic Fury's launch, Qatar's dual-track neutrality was destroyed by the March 2 and March 4 Ras Laffan strikes, and the Saudi backchannel's Larijani link was severed by the March 17 decapitation — and Islamabad's selection carries its own structural limitations, beginning with the fact that Pakistan has no leverage over the IRGCN's autonomous Mosaic Defense provincial commands whose operations are the conflict's primary kinetic driver. The framework's rejection and Iran's sovereignty-maximalist counterproposal have formally documented the complete incompatibility of the two sides' minimum positions at a moment when the Hormuz physical closure timeline, the Arrow-3 depletion trajectory, Chinese resupply logistics, Russian custodianship of supreme leadership authentication, and the War Powers congressional margin are all approaching simultaneous threshold conditions, confirming that any ceasefire architecture must be assembled from actors — Saudi Arabia, China, Russia — who hold leverage over Iranian command structures that neither Araghchi's diplomatic faction nor the US Pakistani channel can reach.

chronicle.headline: US Peace Plan Rejected; Iran Demands Reparations, Hormuz Sovereignty
chronicle.date_label: Day 26 of Operation Epic Fury — March 25, 2026
chronicle.entry:
On March 25, 2026, the United States delivered a 15-point peace framework to Iran through Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, demanding that Tehran permanently dismantle its nuclear program, halt all ballistic missile production, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for beginning discussions about sanctions reduction — three conditions that collectively require Iran to surrender every strategic deterrence instrument it has deployed in the twenty-six days since Operation Epic Fury's launch. Iran's response arrived within hours through the same channel: a formal rejection of all three demands accompanied by a counterproposal requiring US reparations for war damage inflicted since June 2025, a formal American recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz, and acknowledgment of Iran's right to a civilian nuclear program. The exchange occurred against a backdrop of continued military operations on all fronts, with IRGCN autonomous Mosaic Defense commands maintaining the Hormuz mining closure, Israeli ground forces advancing under demolition orders in southern Lebanon, and US carrier aviation conducting coastal suppression missions from the Abraham Lincoln and Gerald R. Ford in the Arabian Sea and Red Sea respectively. Pakistan's willingness to serve as intermediary represents a diplomatic architecture of last resort, assembled after Oman's credibility was destroyed by the February 26 'within reach' juxtaposition with Epic Fury, Qatar's mediation was ended by two Ras Laffan strikes, and the Saudi backchannel's Larijani link was severed by the March 17 decapitation operation.

The human dimension of this exchange is the silence it imposes on the Iranians best positioned to negotiate it. Abbas Araghchi, operating from encrypted relay facilities in Mashhad with degraded communications since the March 17 compromise of the Larijani apparatus, transmitted Iran's counterproposal through Dar without visible authorization from Mojtaba Khamenei's provisional government in Moscow or from Vahidi's IRGC warfighting cell in the Zagros command network — a structural ambiguity that leaves both Pakistani intermediaries and US analysts uncertain whether the counterproposal represents a genuine diplomatic opening or a performance calibrated to prevent Araghchi's faction from being accused of softness by the hardliners who control the autonomous command architecture. Pakistani Foreign Minister Dar is executing this mission aware that his government sits between the world's only two nuclear-armed Muslim states, one of which has just had its supreme leader killed and its Hormuz-adjacent oil infrastructure systematically destroyed, and the other of which is demanding that destruction's beneficiary surrender three strategic instruments simultaneously in exchange for a conversation about sanctions. The Iranian civilian population south of the disrupted supply chains — already enduring 60-percent inflation and fuel rationing from the pre-war crackdown period — is now absorbing the additional economic pressure of having its petrochemical and steel employment infrastructure systematically eliminated, conditions that Araghchi's faction must weigh against the political cost of any concession the warfighting cell can veto.
